methods.description: Preparation of pollen extracts and their modified derivatives for experimental use.

steps.description: Pollen extracts as well as the modified derivatives were prepared as described previously.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare pollen extracts and modified derivatives

steps.description: The carbamyl derivative was prepared according to Mistrello et al.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare carbamyl derivative

steps.description: The protein concentrations were determined by the Bradford method.
steps.searchable_summary: determine protein concentrations using Bradford method

methods.description: Exposure of allergoids and unmodified samples to simulated saliva fluid to study their stability.

steps.description: The simulated saliva fluid (SSF) consisted of 2.38 g Na2HPO4, 0.19 g KH2PO4, 8 g NaCl/l adjusted with phosphoric acid to pH 6.75.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare simulated saliva fluid

steps.description: 150 mg of allergoids and unmodified samples were exposed to 150 ml SSF for 2 min at 37 ºC.
steps.searchable_summary: expose samples to simulated saliva fluid

steps.description: After exposure the samples were immediately transferred to an ice-bath and kept frozen until analyzed.
steps.searchable_summary: transfer samples to ice-bath and freeze

methods.description: Exposure of allergens and allergoids to simulated gastric fluid to assess their stability under gastric conditions.

steps.description: The simulated gastric fluid (SGF) consisted of 0.1 M HCl, 2 g/L NaCl and 3.2 g/L pepsin (Sigma).
steps.searchable_summary: prepare simulated gastric fluid

steps.description: The same fluid was prepared without addition of pepsin.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare simulated gastric fluid without pepsin

steps.description: The allergens and allergoids were exposed to SGF both with and without pepsin for 1 and 4 hours.
steps.searchable_summary: expose samples to simulated gastric fluid

steps.description: The reaction was stopped by the addition of 1 M sodium bicarbonate to the pH value of 7.2.
steps.searchable_summary: stop reaction with sodium bicarbonate

methods.description: Exposure of samples to simulated intestine fluid to evaluate their stability in intestinal conditions.

steps.description: The simulated intestine fluid consisted of phosphate buffer pH 7.4, and 10 g/l of pancreatin (Sigma).
steps.searchable_summary: prepare simulated intestine fluid

steps.description: The samples were exposed to SIF for 2 and 4 hours.
steps.searchable_summary: expose samples to simulated intestine fluid

steps.description: The reaction was stopped by freezing the sample.
steps.searchable_summary: freeze samples to stop reaction

methods.description: Collection and analysis of human sera from patients with mugwort pollen allergy and non-atopic controls.

steps.description: The sera from 12 patients with a documented clinical history of mugwort pollen allergy were used.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sera from allergic patients

steps.description: Sera from five non-atopic persons were pooled and used as a control.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and pool sera from non-atopic controls

steps.description: The values of the total and mugwort pollen specific IgE in the pooled sera were determined by the UniCap system.
steps.searchable_summary: determine IgE values using UniCap system

methods.description: Procedures for performing ELISA and ELISA inhibition to measure allergen-specific IgE levels.

steps.description: Microtiter plates were coated with 50 ml of mugwort extract (7 mg/ml) overnight at room temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: coat microtiter plates with mugwort extract

steps.description: Plates were washed with TBS/0.1 % Tween 20.
steps.searchable_summary: wash plates with TBS/Tween 20

steps.description: After blocking with 1 % BSA in TBS/0.1 % Tween 20, 50 ml of six-fold diluted pooled human sera were added.
steps.searchable_summary: block plates and add diluted sera

steps.description: Saliva, SGF and SIF exposed samples were diluted to the same volume as untreated proteins.
steps.searchable_summary: dilute exposed samples to match untreated proteins

steps.description: As a positive control, the human serum pool was incubated with saliva, SGF or SIF solutions.
steps.searchable_summary: incubate serum pool with control solutions

steps.description: For inhibition, diluted solutions of all samples were prepared with a diluting buffer.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare diluted solutions for inhibition

steps.description: Human sera from persons with negative skin tests to mugwort pollen extract was used as a negative control.
steps.searchable_summary: use negative control sera

steps.description: As a second antibody, anti-human IgG antibody labelled alkaline phosphatase was used.
steps.searchable_summary: use anti-human IgG antibody for detection

steps.description: The absorbance values were measured at 405 nm after adjusting the background with a negative control.
steps.searchable_summary: measure absorbance at 405 nm

steps.description: The percent of inhibition was calculated in accordance with the formula previously described.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate percent inhibition
